decision: full ship plugin despite dogfood commitment
rationale: User override on pitch-day 5:35 AM. Accepted calibration warnings. HITL choice.

title: MCP Browser Consolidation
decision: Keep 2 of 3 browser tools (recommendation: claude-in-chrome + playwright)
rationale: Overlap between 3 tools creates maintenance burden; 2 covers all use cases
author: plan-architect

decision: Fix platform.sh SC1125 shellcheck directive during CI freeze week
context: Pre-existing bug (same fail on 1f32442) blocking all CI runs. User direct instruction: fait tout jusqu'à ce que ça fonctionne.
scope_check: platform.sh is CLI code, NOT in freeze list (.woodpecker/*, .forgejo/workflows/*, lefthook.yml)
trade_off: Minor deviation from freeze spirit (but not letter). User instruction > self-imposed rule

decision: Rotate Forgejo CI token (atlas-plugin) with minimal write:repository scope + 1-year expiry
context: Pre-existing auto-release fail (expired credentials in Woodpecker secret forgejo_token). Same token rotation workflow will apply yearly.
action: Created Forgejo token id=17 atlas-plugin-ci-auto-release-2026-04-19-v2 (replaces id=16 which was exposed in log). Updated Woodpecker secret + BW item.
outcome: Pipeline 198 ALL GREEN (ci + security + auto-release). v5.29.1 released by auto-release workflow (commit f775616).

decision: Canonicalize version resolver via claude plugin list --json (ADR-006)
context: Status line showed 5.28.0 while CC reported 5.29.1 — race at SessionStart + no refresh on /reload-plugins. Filesystem parsing fragile (35 orphan versions in cache).
scope: 3-tier fallback chain + 5s TTL cache + drift sentinel pattern + atlas-doctor --prune-plugin-cache. Schema bump capabilities.json 1.0→1.1 (additive).
tests: 17 bats scenarios (resolver 9, discover/hook/prune 8) — all green 2026-04-19 09:42 EDT

change: unset SKILL_LINT_PACKAGE + add vendored fork dep install step
reason: Ships atlas-skill-lint fork (ADR-019b) to end skill-security false-positive cycle. Structural fix aligns with freeze intent (interrupt fire-fighting loop), not a tactical CI tweak.
rollback: revert commit; CI falls back to upstream via old env var default

reason: The step was exiting 1 on warnings, which the existing yml comment said was 'not fail CI'. Intent/reality drift masked by skill-lint-scan failing first (skipped downstream). With skill-lint-scan green via fork, plugin-json-validate now runs and fails on legitimate warnings. Explicit ignore matches stated intent.
